Name: Azure Database - Export
Description:
Exports a database to a bacpac

*Depends on az cli*

*Source database requires 'Allow Azure services and resources to access this server' option turn on in the SQL server firewall*

Parameters.Name: azDbExport.sqlSvrName
Parameters.Label: SQL Server Name
Parameters.HelpText: The name of the SQL Server to connect to.

Parameters.Name: azDbExport.dbName
Parameters.Label: Database Name
Parameters.HelpText: The name of the database you wish to export

Parameters.Name: azDbExport.adminName
Parameters.Label: SQL Server Admin Name
Parameters.HelpText: The admin name of the SQL server containing the database you wish to export

Parameters.Name: azDbExport.adminPwd
Parameters.Label: Admin Password
Parameters.HelpText: The admin password of the SQL server containing the database you wish to export

Parameters.Name: azDbExport.blobAccessKey
Parameters.Label: Azure Blob Access Key
Parameters.HelpText: the access key (Shared Access Key or Storage Access Key) to grant access to the storage account

Parameters.Name: azDbExport.accessKeyType
Parameters.Label: Access Key Type
Parameters.HelpText: The type of key the access key represents

DisplaySettings.Octopus.SelectOptions:
SharedAccessKey|Shared Access Key
StorageAccessKey|Storage Access Key

Parameters.Name: azDbExport.ContainerUri
Parameters.Label: Container Uri
Parameters.HelpText: The URI of the container to save the exported database in.  Format is: `https://{StorageAccountName}.blob.core.windows.net/{ContainerName}`

Parameters.Name: azDbExport.backupName
Parameters.Label: Backup Name
Parameters.HelpText: The name of the file being saved. The bacpac extension (`.bacpac`) will be appended automatically.  Defaults to DB Name
